title: ceropegia radicans
intro: Ceropegia radicans, also known as the Chain Vine or Devil's Claw, is a type of flowering plant native to tropical regions of Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Ceropegia radicans include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating various gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery. In herbal preparations, the plant's root and tubers are often dried and powdered to be used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture.
